children.heading: Disclosure of financial records not identified with particular customers
children.label: a Disclosure of financial records not identified with particular customers
children.text: Nothing in this chapter prohibits the disclosure of any financial records or information which is not identified with or identifiable as being derived from the financial records of a particular customer.

children.heading: Disclosure to, or examination by, supervisory agency pursuant to exercise of supervisory, regulatory, or monetary functions with respect to financial institutions, holding companies, subsidiaries, institution-affiliated parties, or other persons
children.label: b Disclosure to, or examination by, supervisory agency pursuant to exercise of supervisory, regulatory, or monetary functions with respect to financial institutions, holding companies, subsidiaries, institution-affiliated parties, or other persons
children.text: This chapter shall not apply to the examination by or disclosure to any supervisory agency of financial records or information in the exercise of its supervisory, regulatory, or monetary functions, including conservatorship or receivership functions, with respect to any financial institution, holding company, subsidiary of a financial institution or holding company, institution-affiliated party (within the meaning of ) with respect to a financial institution, holding company, or subsidiary, or other person participating in the conduct of the affairs thereof.section 1813(u) of this title

children.heading: Disclosure pursuant to title 26
children.label: c Disclosure pursuant to title 26
children.text: Nothing in this chapter prohibits the disclosure of financial records in accordance with procedures authorized by title 26.

children.heading: Disclosure pursuant to Federal statute or rule promulgated thereunder
children.label: d Disclosure pursuant to Federal statute or rule promulgated thereunder
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall authorize the withholding of financial records or information required to be reported in accordance with any Federal statute or rule promulgated thereunder.

children.heading: Disclosure pursuant to Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure or comparable rules of other courts
children.label: e Disclosure pursuant to Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure or comparable rules of other courts
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall apply when financial records are sought by a Government authority under the Federal Rules of Civil or Criminal Procedure or comparable rules of other courts in connection with litigation to which the Government authority and the customer are parties.

children.heading: Disclosure pursuant to administrative subpena issued by administrative law judge
children.label: f Disclosure pursuant to administrative subpena issued by administrative law judge
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall apply when financial records are sought by a Government authority pursuant to an administrative subpena issued by an administrative law judge in an adjudicatory proceeding subject to and to which the Government authority and the customer are parties.section 554 of title 5

children.heading: Disclosure pursuant to legitimate law enforcement inquiry respecting name, address, account number, and type of account of particular customers
children.label: g Disclosure pursuant to legitimate law enforcement inquiry respecting name, address, account number, and type of account of particular customers
children.text: The notice requirements of this chapter and sections 3410 and 3412 of this title shall not apply when a Government authority by a means described in and for a legitimate law enforcement inquiry is seeking only the name, address, account number, and type of account of any customer or ascertainable group of customers associated (1) with a financial transaction or class of financial transactions, or (2) with a foreign country or subdivision thereof in the case of a Government authority exercising financial controls over foreign accounts in the United States under ; the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (title II, ) [ et seq.]; or .section 3402 of this titlesection 4305(b) of title 50Public Law 95–22350 U.S.C. 1701section 287c of title 22

children.heading: Disclosure pursuant to lawful proceeding, investigation, etc., directed at financial institution or legal entity or consideration or administration respecting Government loans, loan guarantees, etc.
children.label: h Disclosure pursuant to lawful proceeding, investigation, etc., directed at financial institution or legal entity or consideration or administration respecting Government loans, loan guarantees, etc.
A
children.text: in connection with a lawful proceeding, investigation, examination, or inspection directed at a financial institution (whether or not such proceeding, investigation, examination, or inspection is also directed at a customer) or at a legal entity which is not a customer; or

children.label: B
children.text: in connection with the authority’s consideration or administration of assistance to the customer in the form of a Government loan, loan guaranty, or loan insurance program.

When financial records are sought pursuant to this subsection, the Government authority shall submit to the financial institution the certificate required by . For access pursuant to paragraph (1)(B), no further certification shall be required for subsequent access by the certifying Government authority during the term of the loan, loan guaranty, or loan insurance agreement.section 3403(b) of this title

After the effective date of this chapter, whenever a customer applies for participation in a Government loan, loan guaranty, or loan insurance program, the Government authority administering such program shall give the customer written notice of the authority’s access rights under this subsection. No further notification shall be required for subsequent access by that authority during the term of the loan, loan guaranty, or loan insurance agreement.

children.label: A
children.text: nothing in this paragraph prohibits the use or transfer of a customer’s financial records needed by counsel representing a Government authority in a civil action arising from a Government loan, loan guaranty, or loan insurance agreement; and

children.label: B
children.text: nothing in this paragraph prohibits a Government authority providing assistance to a customer in the form of a loan, loan guaranty, or loan insurance agreement from using or transferring financial records necessary to process, service or foreclose a loan, or to collect on an indebtedness to the Government resulting from a customer’s default.

Notification that financial records obtained pursuant to this subsection may relate to a potential civil, criminal, or regulatory violation by a customer may be given to an agency or department with jurisdiction over that violation, and such agency or department may then seek access to the records pursuant to the provisions of this chapter.

Each financial institution shall keep a notation of each disclosure made pursuant to paragraph (1)(B) of this subsection, including the date of such disclosure and the Government authority to which it was made. The customer shall be entitled to inspect this information.

children.heading: Disclosure pursuant to issuance of subpena or court order respecting grand jury proceeding
children.label: i Disclosure pursuant to issuance of subpena or court order respecting grand jury proceeding
children.text: Nothing in this chapter (except sections 3415 and 3420 of this title) shall apply to any subpena or court order issued in connection with proceedings before a grand jury, except that a court shall have authority to order a financial institution, on which a grand jury subpoena for customer records has been served, not to notify the customer of the existence of the subpoena or information that has been furnished to the grand jury, under the circumstances and for the period specified and pursuant to the procedures established in .section 3409 of this title

children.heading: Disclosure pursuant to proceeding, investigation, etc., instituted by Government Accountability Office and directed at a government authority
children.label: j Disclosure pursuant to proceeding, investigation, etc., instituted by Government Accountability Office and directed at a government authority
children.text: This chapter shall not apply when financial records are sought by the Government Accountability Office pursuant to an authorized proceeding, investigation, examination or audit directed at a government authority.

children.heading: Disclosure necessary for proper administration of programs of certain Government authorities
children.label: k Disclosure necessary for proper administration of programs of certain Government authorities

children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall apply to the disclosure by the financial institution of the name and address of any customer to the Department of the Treasury, the Social Security Administration, or the Railroad Retirement Board, where the disclosure of such information is necessary to, and such information is used solely for the purpose of, the proper administration of , title II of the Social Security Act [ et seq.], or the Railroad Retirement Act of 1974 [ et seq.].section 1441 of title 2642 U.S.C. 40145 U.S.C. 231

children.label: A
children.text: verification of the identity of any person or proper routing and delivery of funds in connection with the issuance of a Federal payment or collection of funds by a Government authority; or

children.label: B
children.text: the investigation or recovery of an improper Federal payment or collection of funds or an improperly negotiated Treasury check.

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a request authorized by paragraph (1) or (2) (and the information contained therein) may be used by the financial institution or its agents solely for the purpose of providing information contained in the financial records of the customer to the Government authority requesting the information, and the financial institution and its agents shall be barred from redisclosure of such information. Any Government authority receiving information pursuant to paragraph (1) or (2) may not disclose or use the information, except for the purposes set forth in such paragraph.

children.heading: Crimes against financial institutions by insiders
children.label: l Crimes against financial institutions by insiders

children.text: any law relating to crimes against financial institutions or supervisory agencies by directors, officers, employees, or controlling shareholders of, or by borrowers from, financial institutions; or
any provision of subchapter II of chapter 53 of title 31 or of section 1956 or 1957 of title 18.

children.heading: Disclosure to, or examination by, employees or agents of Board of Governors of Federal Reserve System or Federal Reserve Bank
children.label: m Disclosure to, or examination by, employees or agents of Board of Governors of Federal Reserve System or Federal Reserve Bank
children.text: This chapter shall not apply to the examination by or disclosure to employees or agents of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System or any Federal Reserve Bank of financial records or information in the exercise of the Federal Reserve System’s authority to extend credit to the financial institutions or others.

children.heading: Disclosure to, or examination by, Resolution Trust Corporation or its employees or agents
children.label: n Disclosure to, or examination by, Resolution Trust Corporation or its employees or agents
children.text: This chapter shall not apply to the examination by or disclosure to the Resolution Trust Corporation or its employees or agents of financial records or information in the exercise of its conservatorship, receivership, or liquidation functions with respect to a financial institution.

children.heading: Disclosure to, or examination by, Federal Housing Finance Agency or Federal home loan banks
children.label: o Disclosure to, or examination by, Federal Housing Finance Agency or Federal home loan banks
children.text: This chapter shall not apply to the examination by or disclosure to the Federal Housing Finance Agency or any of the Federal home loan banks of financial records or information in the exercise of the Federal Housing Finance Agency’s authority to extend credit (either directly or through a Federal home loan bank) to financial institutions or others.

children.heading: Access to information necessary for administration of certain veteran benefits laws
children.label: p Access to information necessary for administration of certain veteran benefits laws

children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall apply to the disclosure by the financial institution of the name and address of any customer to the Department of Veterans Affairs where the disclosure of such information is necessary to, and such information is used solely for the purposes of, the proper administration of benefits programs under laws administered by the Secretary.

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any request authorized by paragraph (1) (and the information contained therein) may be used by the financial institution or its agents solely for the purpose of providing the customer’s name and address to the Department of Veterans Affairs and shall be barred from redisclosure by the financial institution or its agents.

children.heading: Disclosure pursuant to Federal contractor-issued travel charge card
children.label: q Disclosure pursuant to Federal contractor-issued travel charge card
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall apply to the disclosure of any financial record or information to a Government authority in conjunction with a Federal contractor-issued travel charge card issued for official Government travel.

children.heading: Disclosure to the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection
children.label: r Disclosure to the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall apply to the examination by or disclosure to the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection of financial records or information in the exercise of its authority with respect to a financial institution.
